metadata.title: GardenOS — Smart garden & robot mowing
metadata.description: mygardenos.com: digitized gardens and robot mowing subscriptions.

home.headline:
Digital Gardens.
Smarter Mowing.
Unbeatable Price.
home.sub: GardenOS unifies mapping, sensing, and operations data with a subscription-first robot mowing service—less guesswork, more predictable yards.

col1.title: End-to-end garden digitization
col1.body: From boundary mapping to growth and fleet signals—one digital twin and dashboard so every decision is grounded in live context.

col2.title: Mowing as a subscription
col2.body: Seasonal or monthly plans with routing, scheduling, and remote ops included—less weekend rescue, more reliable coverage.

col3.title: Built for homes and portfolios
col3.body: The same stack scales from single yards to multi-parcel estates with repeatable rollout, monitoring, and operations playbooks.

prototype.title: Next public test window opens soon
prototype.body: We will run live mowing passes, boundary regression, and edge-case drills on a published schedule—RSVP for reminders and send your yard checklist.

registerBlock.title: Join early membership, hold your subscription perks
registerBlock.body: Share your contact to get release notes, pilot slots, and launch pricing bands—we also post the roadmap in the community.

about.metaDescription: Mission, vision, problems we solve, and values behind GardenOS digitized gardens and subscription mowing.
about.title: About GardenOS
about.lead: GardenOS serves homes and property teams with a combined playbook: digitize the yard first, then deliver mowing through a subscription-grade robot service.

mission.title: Mission
mission.body: Lower the operational bar for high-quality outdoor care—sensing, data, and robotics delivered as a subscription you can plan around.

vision.title: Vision
vision.body: Become a trusted standard for digitized gardens and outdoor spaces—connecting devices, service partners, and communities into a durable smart-outdoors network.

p1.title: Fragmented knowledge, hero-dependent ops
p1.body: Boundaries, slopes, vegetation, and fleet health live across paper trails and chats—hard to audit or hand off.

p2.title: Mowing is costly to scale
p2.body: Outsourced crews fluctuate; owned robots idle without maintenance discipline—few repeatable service standards or data loops.

p3.title: Subscriptions need a trustworthy backbone
p3.body: Moving from one-off sales to recurring care demands transparent job logs, predictable windows, and accountable remote operations.

v1.title: Safety and compliance first
v1.body: Outdoor robotics must respect boundaries, privacy, and local rules before feature velocity.

v2.title: Data should serve the owner
v2.body: Mapping and mowing telemetry stay within the scope you authorize for optimization—not a lock-in ransom.

v3.title: Co-build with the community
v3.body: Early users, property partners, and builders shape the roadmap with grounded field feedback.

cta.title: Exploring pilots or partnerships?
cta.body: Start with how to participate, or create a membership to get updates and invites.

participate.metaDescription: Three ways to engage, baseline requirements, and sample cadence for GardenOS pilots and co-creation.
participate.title: Join the GardenOS early build
participate.lead: Homeowners, property operators, and technical partners can all contribute—the paths below turn your signal into roadmap decisions.

step1.title: Early access & private betas
step1.body: Exercise mapping tools, the mobile app, and prototype mowing flows—then file structured feedback and tickets inside published windows.

step2.title: Scenario workshops
step2.body: Join virtual or on-site workshops to share edge cases so we can prioritize acceptance tests and release notes.

step3.title: Pilot rollouts & joint ops
step3.body: When aligned, bring pilot parcels or project-based collaborations to validate subscription delivery, SLAs, and settlement models.

participate.reqIntro: Safety and data quality gates differ by track—the bullets below are universal baselines (see each cohort invite for specifics).
participate.req1: You can sign the relevant pilot / beta agreement and authorize on-site mapping plus job telemetry needed to improve the product.
participate.req2: Sites need baseline network and power; disclose complex boundaries or local restrictions up front.
participate.req3: Please use the feedback template (screenshots, repro steps, expected behavior) so engineering can respond quickly.
participate.req4: Livestream schedules may shift—follow this page or member email for the final calendar.
participate.scheduleBody: Q2: prototype revisit + boundary regression week. Q3: expand pilot metros and billing dry-runs. Official dates publish on Live & testing.
participate.ctaBody: RSVP for the next public test, or create a membership to hold launch perks and notifications.

live.metaDescription: Public prototype windows, how to watch, replays, and release notes for GardenOS field tests.
live.title: Livestreams, replays, and release notes
live.lead: Track public prototype windows, how to watch, and what changed in each drop—we ship on a predictable, traceable cadence.
live.windowBody: We demo typical mowing passes, boundary hugging, obstacle avoidance, and recovery flows—with live Q&A for your scenarios.
live.windowNote: Once dates and RSVP links are firm, we broadcast via member email and pinned community posts; replay links land here.
live.channelsTitle: How to watch and download assets

ch1.title: Live broadcast
ch1.body: Public sessions include multi-camera streams and captions—join five minutes early for the safety briefing.

ch2.title: Replays & write-ups
ch2.body: Edited replays and written recaps ship within 48 hours, including parameter ranges and known limitations.

ch3.title: Technical briefs
ch3.body: Each release ships with a changelog and migration notes; breaking behavior is called out explicitly.

rel1.title: v0.4 — Subscription billing preview
rel1.body: Draft pricing by parcel size and cadence with exportable reconciliation reports (test data).

rel2.title: v0.3 — Boundary regression toolkit
rel2.body: Compare remapping deltas and auto-generate review task lists.

rel3.title: v0.2 — Remote ops console
rel3.body: Searchable job logs, explainable fault codes, and remote safe-stop controls.

live.bannerBody: Create a membership and enable email alerts to get RSVP and watch links the moment a schedule drops.

register.metaDescription: Create a GardenOS membership for updates, pilots, and launch pricing—lead form opens soon.
register.title: Create your GardenOS membership
register.lead: Share contact details to receive release notes, pilot invites, launch pricing bands, and event reminders. The form below is a visual scaffold—submission will open once backend and compliance checks are wired.

b1.title: Product updates & roadmap
b1.body: Regular digests on shipped features, known issues, and upcoming milestones.

b2.title: Pilot and event alerts
b2.body: Get first notice when cohorts open; some sessions are members-only.

b3.title: Launch and subscription conversations
b3.body: During pricing dry-runs, join surveys or interviews for a shot at early bands or service bundles (per published rules).

s1.title: Share basics
s1.body: Email, display name, and optional org context for notifications and verification.

s2.title: Choose channels
s2.body: Pick email and/or community channels—update preferences anytime via the footer link.

s3.title: Receive targeted invites
s3.body: When pilots or livestreams open, you get the RSVP link and briefing pack.

register.formHint: Placeholder layout only; submissions will connect to our API and consent flows when ready.
register.privacy: We handle your data under our privacy policy—product and pilot comms only, never sold lists.
register.sideBody: Skim About and How to participate before choosing a track.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The link may be outdated or this page is not available yet.
